about.bio: Design director obsessed with the space between user research and product strategy. I help teams build things people actually want to use.

connectionPath.role: Head of Design · The Trusted List

openRequests.title: Feedback on our new onboarding redesign
openRequests.description: We've just shipped a major rework of our onboarding flow and I'd love fresh eyes before we run a wider usability study. Looking for honest critique on clarity and visual hierarchy.

openRequests.title: Intro to a PM who's worked on marketplace products
openRequests.description: Building out a marketplace feature and want to talk to someone who's been through this before — particularly around trust and fraud at scale.

items.feedbackSubject: Sam thinks this request worked out!
items.feedbackBody: Jen was incredibly generous with her time. She reviewed my research plan with surgical precision — caught gaps I'd completely overlooked and reframed the core questions in a way that made everything click. Her instinct for what actually matters in user research is rare.

items.requestTitle: Help us define our design principles
items.feedbackSubject: Marcus thinks this request worked out!
items.feedbackBody: Jen brought a level of clarity and structure to our design principles workshop that completely changed the outcome. She asked the questions our team had been avoiding and helped us arrive at principles that actually felt true to who we are.

items.requestTitle: Critique my portfolio before senior interviews
items.feedbackSubject: Priya thinks this partially worked out
items.feedbackBody: Really honest and specific feedback that I could act on immediately. Jen doesn't just tell you what's wrong — she explains the underlying principle so you can fix it yourself next time. That's the kind of mentorship that actually sticks.

recommendations.body: Jen has an extraordinary ability to make complex design problems feel approachable. She helped me rethink our entire onboarding flow in a single conversation — the kind of clarity that usually takes weeks to arrive at. Anyone lucky enough to have her in their circle should ask her everything.

Working with Jen is what I imagine it would be like to have a senior partner to think out loud with. She's got great instincts and even better questions. Every conversation ends with me seeing the problem differently.

Jen is the kind of design leader who makes everyone around her better — not through instruction but through the quality of her thinking. She gave me the most useful portfolio feedback I've ever received and did it in under 20 minutes.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What help do people come to you for?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: Usually design critique and research strategy. People bring me half-formed ideas or work that isn't landing and want to understand why. I'm good at diagnosing root causes rather than just describing symptoms — which is the part that's actually hard.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What problems do you love solving?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: The ones where the product problem and the people problem are the same problem. When you trace a confusing UX back to a fuzzy team decision, or a high churn rate back to misaligned priorities — that's where I want to spend my time. I really love diving into these type of issues with engineers and PMs and working together as a team to solve it. It may not always be glamorous, but it's where the real impact is made.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What does a great work day look like?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: A morning doing actual design work with no interruptions. A long lunch walk. An afternoon of reviews and conversation where I feel like I'm genuinely useful rather than just present. Finishing the day with something tangible to show for it.

jobs.title: Design Director
jobs.description: Leading a team of 12 designers across product and brand. Responsible for design system, research operations, and strategic design partnerships.

jobs.title: Senior Product Designer
jobs.description: Embedded design partner for B2B SaaS clients. Worked alongside Ryan Shafer on three major product launches.

jobs.title: Product Designer
jobs.description: Designed patient-facing mobile experiences for a digital health platform serving 500K users.

about.bio: Product leader who's spent 15 years building B2B tools people actually use at work. I care deeply about organisational design as much as product design.

connectionPath.role: Head of Design · The Trusted List

openRequests.title: Intro to a Head of Design open to a VP-partnership role
openRequests.description: Growing a product org and want to partner with a design leader who thinks in systems. Looking for warm intros rather than cold outreach.

items.requestTitle: How do I make the case for more design headcount?
items.feedbackSubject: Jen thinks this request worked out!
items.feedbackBody: Marcus gave me the clearest framework I've ever heard for making the business case for design investment. He's been on both sides of the table and it shows — practical, direct, and exactly what I needed to go into that conversation prepared.

items.requestTitle: Help me navigate a messy stakeholder situation
items.feedbackSubject: Ryan thinks this request worked out!
items.feedbackBody: I've worked alongside a lot of product leaders, and Marcus stands out for how grounded his instincts are. He doesn't just give opinions — he asks the right questions first. His perspective helped me see the path through a situation I'd been stuck on for weeks.

recommendations.body: Marcus is one of those rare product leaders who makes design better without being a designer. He understands what great design is for and makes room for it. If you're a designer who wants to work with someone who genuinely gets it, Marcus is your person.

Marcus helped me reframe a difficult product decision I'd been overthinking for weeks. In one conversation he cut through all the noise and helped me see what actually mattered. He has a gift for simplifying without losing the important nuance.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What help do people come to you for?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: Career navigation and org design, mostly. How to structure a product team, how to influence up, how to make decisions with incomplete information. I've been a PM, a manager, and an executive — so I can usually see a situation from multiple angles at once.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What problems do you love solving?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: The messy people-and-process problems that product leaders often can't talk about publicly. When a team is stuck not because of strategy but because of some unspoken dynamic — that's where I want to help.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What does a great work day look like?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: Making at least one person's job materially easier. A hard decision well made. An honest conversation that needed to happen. Good food and getting outside at some point. Simple things.

jobs.title: VP of Product
jobs.description: Leading product for a B2B communication platform. Grew the team from 4 to 18 PMs and redesigned the product planning process end-to-end.

jobs.title: Director of Product
jobs.description: Owned the core product roadmap. Launched the notification and inbox systems used by 2M+ users.

jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
jobs.description: PM for enterprise accounts, managing multi-million dollar product contracts across media and retail sectors.

about.bio: I help organisations understand the gap between what they think their product does and what it actually does to people. Systems thinker, service designer, recovering academic.

connectionPath.role: Head of Design · The Trusted List

openRequests.title: Looking for a Head of Design role in Europe
openRequests.description: Ready for my next step into design leadership. Looking for warm intros at companies that take design seriously at the executive level — particularly in fintech, health, or civic tech.

openRequests.title: Peer review on a service blueprint
openRequests.description: Working on a complex service design engagement and want a second set of eyes on a blueprint before I present to the client. Looking for someone who's done this at scale.

items.requestTitle: Help me think through a career pivot
items.feedbackSubject: Ryan thinks this request worked out!
items.feedbackBody: Anika is the person I go to when I need to think through something that doesn't have a clean answer. She's thoughtful, direct, and genuinely cares about helping — not just being seen as helpful. That's a rare combination.

items.feedbackSubject: Jen thinks this request worked out!
items.feedbackBody: Anika has a rare ability to see structure in messy qualitative data. She helped me rebuild our entire synthesis process in a way that actually scales. I've recommended her to three other teams since.

recommendations.body: Anika thinks at a level of abstraction that most designers can't reach. She helped me see the systemic reasons behind a product problem I'd been treating as a UX issue. If you're working on something complex and interconnected, she's exactly who you want in the room.

I've worked with a lot of strategists. Anika is one of the few who can hold the big picture and the fine detail simultaneously — and she makes both legible to everyone in the room, regardless of their background.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What help do people come to you for?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: Making sense of complexity. When a product or a team is stuck and nobody can quite name why, I help surface the underlying structure. Whether that's a service blueprint, a systems map, or just a conversation that cuts through the noise.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What problems do you love solving?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: The ones that turn out to be several simpler problems wearing a coat. Most 'wicked' problems in organisations are actually a cluster of easier problems nobody's had the courage or the space to disentangle. That's where I want to be.

inTheirOwnWords.question: What does a great work day look like?
inTheirOwnWords.answer: Thinking hard in the morning. Teaching or sharing in the afternoon. A walk somewhere green in between. Ending the day with something I didn't understand when I started.

jobs.title: Principal UX Strategist

jobs.title: Senior Service Designer
